selection.rule: Select the highest default-weight check in each catalog domain. When checks tie, select the lowest numeric check id.

protocol.publication: Publish hits, misses, false positives, severity accuracy, clean-control rate, token cost, and elapsed time in ACCURACY.md. Never publish a skill-lift number without complete paired arms.

legacy_pilot.reason: The original capture did not record model or harness and has no installed-skill arm.

targets.seed: The product artifact names activation and retention success metrics, but no shipped event or aggregation can compute either metric.
targets.clean_control: A neighboring metric has an explicit event-level definition and a live emitting call site.

targets.seed: A multi-tenant mutation relies on one route-local tenant check, with no second isolation layer and one sibling path outside the guard.
targets.clean_control: Authentication and network-edge boundaries have two independent enforcement layers.

targets.seed: A pinned direct production dependency is archived and has been silent for more than 18 months at the fixture's evidence date.
targets.clean_control: A superficially old direct dependency remains maintained and supported at the pinned version.

targets.seed: An orders.customer_id column and ORM association exist without a database foreign key in the shipped migrations.
targets.clean_control: A disposable sessions.user_id relationship has a correctly typed foreign key and intentional cascade.

targets.seed: The password-reset route is mounted before its rate limiter and compares reset tokens with ordinary string equality.
targets.clean_control: The login route uses a supported password KDF and is mounted behind its limiter.

targets.seed: A model-returned boolean is parsed from JSON without schema validation and directly authorizes a privileged action.
targets.clean_control: A neighboring extraction path validates strict structured output before using it.

targets.seed: The sign-in form blocks paste into the password field while a nearby profile form permits normal keyboard and autofill behavior.
targets.clean_control: The core recovery flow remains keyboard reachable and preserves focus order.

targets.seed: The primary application shell has a fixed 1200px width and forces horizontal scrolling at a 320px viewport.
targets.clean_control: A sibling public page uses fluid containers, reserved media dimensions, and non-hover controls.

targets.seed: An indexable product route emits an empty server shell and adds its body, title, canonical, and JSON-LD only after client hydration.
targets.clean_control: A sibling indexable route returns complete metadata and body content in initial HTML.

targets.seed: The README names a removed start command and omits a required production environment variable used by the entry point.
targets.clean_control: Build and test commands in the same README match package scripts.

targets.seed: One load-bearing module has narration comments at more than three times the representative sample's comment density.
targets.clean_control: Neighboring modules use sparse comments that explain decisions rather than restating code.

targets.seed: A repository claiming Pillars compliance has agents/context.md marked present but stubbed and agents/repo.md missing.
targets.clean_control: A scoped optional pillar is intentionally excluded with a reason and does not stand in for either floor pillar.

targets.seed: A gitleaks configuration exists but no pre-commit or CI command loads it, creating an unwired paper control.
targets.clean_control: A pre-push hook explicitly rejects force pushes to protected branches.

targets.seed: Theme tokens are declared, but the only rendered primary component retains the framework's unmodified default theme and hard-coded colors.
targets.clean_control: A sibling component inherits the committed primary-color token and uses the installed icon library.

targets.seed: Scheduled roadmap rows are bare feature names such as SSO and dark mode, with no outcome, appetite, or owner.
targets.clean_control: A neighboring row is outcome-framed and names its checkpoint and owner.

targets.seed: One populated-table migration adds a required replacement column, backfills it, and drops the old column in the same deploy.
targets.clean_control: A neighboring schema change separates expand and contract with an explicit cutover.

targets.seed: A request metric sends user_id and request_id as labels to a per-series backend with no exclusion rule.
targets.clean_control: Trace identifiers remain in logs and spans, not metric labels.

targets.seed: The landing hero remains equally plausible after substituting two direct competitor names and states no falsifiable differentiator.
targets.clean_control: A supporting positioning note names the audience and replacement but is not enough to repair the generic hero.
